metadata.title: Team AI-Use Register: One Shared Boundary
metadata.description_blurb: Individual caution does not aggregate into team safety — one shared register with eight fields converts informal AI practice into an auditable boundary that everyone on the team can read.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B06: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: Five people on the marketing team each used Claude carefully. Different surfaces, different use cases, different rules each had internalized. No shared model of what data could go in, which outputs needed review, who owned the errors. When a Cowork connector was added for one team member, it was available to all of them — no one had agreed to that scope. Individual caution had not become team safety. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, fill out team AI-use register entry

props.command: Help me fill out a team AI-use register entry for our weekly report workflow.

beats.narration_text: Individual caution does not aggregate into team safety. Five cautious practitioners with different informal rules produce inconsistent practice, accountability gaps, and governance drift. When one person connects a shared asset — an MCP server, a connector, a project with broad file access — it becomes available to everyone on the team. The scope of that decision was not agreed to by anyone except the person who clicked.
beats.new_visual_element: Individual ≠ team — five cautious people / no shared boundary / connector added = team scope expanded

beats.narration_text: One register entry has eight fields. Use case: what this workflow does. Owner: who is accountable. Surface: which Claude tool is used. Data allowed: what input may be provided. Data forbidden: what may not go in. Human gate: who reviews what before output is used. Log: where the task record is kept. Escalation: what happens when something unexpected occurs. Eight fields. One entry per use case. Readable by everyone on the team.
beats.new_visual_element: Register fields — use case / owner / surface / data allowed / data forbidden / gate / log / escalation
beats.spark_line: Eight fields. One entry per use case.

beats.narration_text: Nominal oversight is not oversight. A reviewer who lacks time, context, authority, or evidence cannot catch errors. Meaningful oversight requires four things: time to check, relevant context, authority to act on what is found, and an artifact to check against. The register entry names the human gate and what it checks. Without that specification, human review is a formality, not a control.
beats.new_visual_element: Meaningful oversight — time / context / authority / artifact / nominal oversight has none of these

beats.narration_text: The minimum viable register is one entry per use case, agreed on by the team. Not formality — one boundary document that tells every team member what data goes in, what comes out, who checks it, and what to do when it goes wrong. The register does not require a governance committee. It requires fifteen minutes and a shared doc. Don't oversell it as compliance. Sell it as: 'at least agree on the boundary.'
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — sample register entry / eight fields / one use case / shared and readable
beats.spark_line: At least agree on the boundary.

beats.narration_text: Draft your first register entry with this prompt. Paste this.

props.command: Help me write a team AI-use register entry for this workflow: [describe it]. Fill in: (1) Use case — what it does, (2) Owner — who is accountable, (3) Surface — which Claude tool, (4) Data allowed — what may go in, (5) Data forbidden — what may not, (6) Human gate — who reviews what before output is used, (7) Log — where the task record lives, (8) Escalation — what happens when something unexpected occurs.

beats.narration_text: If this gives the team one shared boundary it did not have before, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
